text.title: Partnership Strategy Five-Stage Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-partnership-strategy` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Name the partnership type explicitly: affiliate, co-marketing, integration, reseller, or white-label. Vague 'partnership' framing is forbidden in any deal record.

rationale.source: Business-development practice (HubSpot Partner Programs, Salesforce AppExchange playbooks)

rule.rationale: Vague partnership language hides misaligned expectations. Naming the type pins the operating motion (commission vs joint content vs technical integration).

rule.statement: Score every candidate partnership on five axes: reach, relevance, trust, effort, risk. Pursuing a partnership that scores low on any axis without written justification is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unscored partnerships drift into vanity deals (big logo, no reach into ICP). The 5-axis rubric forces deliberate selection.

rule.statement: The deal record explicitly states the value to BOTH sides — what the partner gets and what the operator gets. One-sided value statements ('this will be great for them') are forbidden.

rationale.source: Partnership negotiation practice (Blair Enns, Patrick McKenzie)

rule.rationale: Partnerships that lack explicit mutual value collapse at the first conflict. Writing both sides forces the operator to test whether the deal is real.

rule.statement: Outreach messages lead with the partner's benefit in the FIRST paragraph. Leading with self-benefit ('we'd love to partner with you') is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Partner-benefit-first outreach gets 3-5x the response rate of self-benefit-first. The constraint is the dominant variable in outreach performance.

rule.statement: Schedule 30 / 60 / 90 day reviews at deal signing with named outcomes per checkpoint (leads exchanged, integration shipped, revenue attributed). Partnerships without scheduled reviews are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Most partnerships die from drift, not from explicit termination. Pre-scheduled reviews force a yes/no decision before drift becomes permanent.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
